evidence.snippet:
##### Uncrewed Systems
We offer uncrewed aerial vehicles (UAVs) with mission-driven technology and flexibility. Uncrewed aircraft systems from Redwire can be deployed quickly and easily, providing rapid response for time-critical operations.
Redwire Announces $20 Million in Follow-On Orders from the Marine Corps to Deliver Stalker UAS Advanced Navigation and Standard Systems

items.notes: Stalker Block 30, Stalker Block 40, Penguin C MK2, Penguin C VTOL are complete UAS platforms. VXE30 Stalker fleet exceeds 200 aircraft in USMC/Navy service.

evidence.snippet:
##### Octopus ISR and Optics Payloads
Our Octopus gimbal solutions deliver the intelligence, surveillance, and reconnaissance that drives mission success. Our optronic surveillance systems are developed for either crewed or uncrewed systems and offer robust capabilities – no matter the mission or vehicle.
LEARN MORE

items.notes: Octopus EO/IR gimbal family (E95, E140 MWIR, E140LC, E140Z G2, E180) developed for crewed or uncrewed systems.

evidence.snippet: Propane-powered fuel cells are an effective backup source for redundant power solutions. Our energy systems are proven in freight rail, aviation, monitoring, UAV, and other critical applications when downtime is not an option

items.notes: Propane fuel cell power systems (Endurance Series, Performer Series) proven for UAV applications.

headquarters.notes: Jacksonville is Redwire corporate HQ. Edge Autonomy (acquired by Redwire) has operational sites in San Luis Obispo CA and Ann Arbor MI. The edgeautonomy.io domain redirects entirely to rdw.com.

evidence.snippet: Redwire Announces $20 Million in Follow-On Orders from the Marine Corps to Deliver Stalker UAS Advanced Navigation and Standard Systems
Redwire has been contracted directly by the Ukraine Armed Forces to deliver Penguin UAS for advanced combat-proven reconnaissance, surveillance, and target acquisition capabilities.

##### Uncrewed Systems
We offer uncrewed aerial vehicles (UAVs) with mission-driven technology and flexibility. Uncrewed aircraft systems from Redwire can be deployed quickly and easily, providing rapid response for time-critical operations.

drone_supply_chain_role.notes: Edge Autonomy (now Redwire Defense Tech) designs and manufactures complete UAS platforms — Stalker sUAS (200+ aircraft fleet for USMC/Navy) and Penguin C family (delivered to Ukraine Armed Forces for RSTA). Also produces ISR payloads and fuel-cell power systems as subsystems.

items.name: Endurance Series
items.product_type: UAV propane fuel cell power system

items.name: Performer Series
items.product_type: UAV propane fuel cell power system

products.notes: Individual product pages (Stalker Block 30/40, Penguin C MK2/VTOL, all Octopus ISR models) were in crawl_index but skipped due to budget. Product list derived from the product archive summary page. Additional Penguin variants (MK2-5 VTOL, MK2X) found in crawl_index but not in the fetched product archive.

evidence.snippet: Group 2 / NATO Class I and non-ITAR, compliant to both MOD payload and cursor-on-target
Group 3 / NATO Class I and Non-ITAR with MISB compliant video stream and voice and data relay for Silvus tactical radios

ndaa.notes: Products claim 'Non-ITAR' and described as 'U.S.-built defense technologies' but no explicit NDAA Section 848/889 compliance claim found

blue_uas.notes: No Blue UAS or Blue sUAS mention found in any fetched page

evidence.snippet: Redwire Announces $20 Million in Follow-On Orders from the Marine Corps to Deliver Stalker UAS Advanced Navigation and Standard Systems
Redwire has been contracted directly by the Ukraine Armed Forces to deliver Penguin UAS for advanced combat-proven reconnaissance, surveillance, and target acquisition capabilities.

readiness.notes: Active USMC/Navy production contracts for Stalker sUAS ($20M follow-on order). Penguin UAS delivered to Ukraine Armed Forces. Product archive lists 4 catalog Uncrewed Systems SKUs.

unresolved_questions: Whether VXE30 Stalker is the Block 30 or Block 40 production variant
Whether Penguin MK2-5 VTOL and Penguin MK2X are current production variants
Whether Edge Autonomy/Redwire holds NDAA Section 848 compliance certification for Stalker or Penguin UAS

fetch_requests.reason: Product specs for Stalker Block 30 sUAS — would provide range, endurance, payload capacity, and confirm relationship to VXE30 Stalker designation.
Product specs for Stalker Block 40 sUAS — would clarify differences from Block 30 and current program-of-record variant.
Product specs for Penguin C MK2 fixed-wing UAS — would confirm airframe specs and deployment roles.
Product specs for Penguin C VTOL variant — would confirm VTOL specifications and whether navigation products are embedded.
News article about Edge Autonomy team at AUSA 2025 — would confirm product line breadth and possibly surface NDAA compliance information.

tagline: Manufactures fixed-wing small UAS (Stalker, Penguin series) and electro-optical ISR payloads (Octopus gimbal family) for long-endurance reconnaissance, plus propane fuel-cell power systems and spacecraft platforms (Mako, SabreSat). Marine Corps and Ukraine have fielded Stalker systems; LR/LE program of record ongoing. NDAA compliance and Blue UAS certification status unknown. Now operating under Redwire Corporation as of recent acquisition.
